summary: Iteration 170 - Frontend testing for Occasion Box Builder visibility and mobile experience after MemberDashboard refactoring. Found and fixed critical bug in CelebratePage.jsx where 'navigate' was not defined. All mobile navigation and occasion box routes working correctly.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All occasion box and mobile navigation features working correctly. Dashboard still crashes Playwright due to heavy content but loads fine in real browsers (expected behavior).

occasion_box_birthday_route.details: /occasion-box/birthday loads correctly with Birthday Box builder, shows Step 1 of 4: Birthday Cake with product selection

occasion_box_gotcha_day_route.details: /occasion-box/gotcha-day loads correctly with Gotcha Day Box builder, shows Step 1 of 3: Celebration Treats

celebrate_page_build_box_button.details: Build Birthday Box button on /celebrate page now correctly navigates to /occasion-box/birthday. Fixed missing useNavigate import.

mobile_navigation_bar.details: Fixed bottom navigation bar present on all tested pages (Home, Services/Care, Shop, Celebrate, Occasion Box pages) with HOME, SERVICES, ORDERS, PROFILE and Mira button in center

shop_page_pillar_filters.details: Shop page shows pillar filters (All Pillars, Celebrate, Dine, Stay, Travel, Care, Enjoy, Fit, Learn, Shop) and product count (615 Products • 14 Pillars)

login_and_dashboard.details: Login with email/password works correctly, redirects to /dashboard. Dashboard crashes Playwright during screenshot due to page size (expected per test notes)

pets_api_with_celebrations.details: /api/pets returns pets with birth_date and gotcha_date fields for MyCelebrations component

celebrate_page_navigate_error.issue: ReferenceError: navigate is not defined - handleBuildBox function used navigate() but useNavigate was not imported
celebrate_page_navigate_error.fix: Added useNavigate to imports from react-router-dom and called const navigate = useNavigate() in component
